data.title: Swing whale nemorino.eth bought back 3000 WETH that it sold yesterday at a price of $2991.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Ai Yi, the whale nemorino.eth sold 3,000 WETH at $3,095 last night and bought them all back at $2,991.29 just 5 minutes ago, effectively trading to lower his average price. He currently holds a total of 7,104.13 WTH, with an average cost of $3,045.24, and is still experiencing a floating loss of $390,000.

data.title: A wallet associated with the Pudgy Penguins team deposited 397 million PENGU into Binance.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens, a wallet associated with the Pudgy Penguins team deposited 397 million PENGU, worth $4.02 million, into Binance.

data.title: Brother Machi has lost approximately $4.16 million this week, but he is still holding onto his long positions in Ethereum.
data.description: Arkham posted on the X platform that Machi Big Brother Ben Huang Licheng has lost $4.16 million on Hyperliquid this week, bringing his total profit and loss to a new low of -$24.5 million. However, he is still holding long positions in Ethereum, with a current position value of about $1.7 million and a floating profit of about $20,000.

data.title: The Central Bank of Iran received over $500 million in USDT last year to support its currency exchange rate.
data.description: Research by blockchain intelligence firm Elliptic indicates that the Central Bank of Iran acquired over $500 million in USDT last year. All confirmed USDT has since left wallets linked to Iran, and the report suggests the Central Bank used this stablecoin to back the price of the Iranian rial. Tom Robinson, co-founder and chief scientist at Elliptic, stated that leaked documents detail purchases made through an entity called Modex, which he believes may be a cryptocurrency broker willing to transact with the Iranian government. (Decrypt)

data.title: Rising JGB Yields and Tariff Tensions Push Bitcoin into Defensive Mode, Says Analyst
data.description:
Bitcoin declined more than 6% over the past week as Japanese government bond yields climbed to 2. 29%, the highest since 1999, while escalating U. S. –Europe trade tensions over Greenland tariffs tightened financial conditions and eroded risk appetite across global markets.
source: https://cryptonews.com/news/rising-jgb-yields-and-tariff-tensions-push-bitcoin-into-defensive-mode-says-analyst/

data.title: Iran's Central Bank Acquired $507M in Tether’s USDT Stablecoin: Elliptic
data.description:
The Central Bank of Iran no longer holds any of the flagged USDT, after using it to support the rial and settle international transactions.
source: https://decrypt.co/355277/irans-central-bank-acquired-507m-in-tethers-usdt-stablecoin-elliptic

data.title: XRP Max Pain Hits $1.90 – Shakeout Before Moon?
data.description:
XRP fell to $1. 90 amid renewed tariff threats and CLARITY Act delays, triggering fresh capitulation among holders, but some analysts view this as a coordinated shakeout, with technical compression signaling a high-probability breakout. As institutions test assets before scaling adoption, traders are wondering if this is the dip that positions believers for the next leg […] The post XRP Max Pain Hits $1
source: https://www.techgaged.com/xrp-max-pain-hits-1-90-shakeout-before-moon/

data.title: SEC Crypto Task Force Pressed on Self-Custody Rights and DeFi ‘Dealer’ Rules in New Filings
data.description:
Two new filings to the SEC Crypto Task Force call for clear rules on self-custody and DeFi trading as Congress debates federal crypto legislation amid delays in CLARITY Act.
source: https://cryptonews.com/news/sec-crypto-task-force-self-custody-defi-dealer-rules/

data.title: XRP Escapes Chart Death Sentence at $1.8261 With -88% Scenario Rejected
data.description:
XRP dipped below the key $1. 8896 "lifeline" today, triggering a brief -88% collapse setup, but it snapped back fast, leaving bears hanging and bulls hoping this was not just a fakeout.
source: https://u.today/xrp-escapes-chart-death-sentence-at-18261-with-88-scenario-rejected

data.title: BlackRock Identifies Cryptocurrencies as Key Market Influencers by 2026
data.description: BlackRock, the world's largest asset manager with $10 trillion under management, has identified cryptocurrencies such as Bitcoin, Ether, and stablecoins as pivotal themes impacting markets by 2026. According to NS3.AI, the firm emphasizes blockchain technology and tokenization as transformative elements in the financial sector. This perspective underscores the increasing institutional acknowledgment of digital assets' expanding influence within the global financial system.

data.title: PAXG Tokens Transferred from Null Address to Paxos
data.description: At 23:33, a total of 5,301.44 PAXG tokens were transferred from a Null Address to Paxos. According to ChainCatcher, this transaction involved a significant amount of PAXG, highlighting ongoing movements within the cryptocurrency market. The transfer underscores the dynamic nature of digital asset transactions and the importance of monitoring such activities for market insights.

data.title: U.S. Considers Greenland Acquisition Amid Dollar Weakness and Rising Metal Prices
data.description:
The United States is considering the strategic acquisition of Greenland, a region abundant in rare earth minerals essential for technological advancements. According to NS3.AI, this move comes as the U.S. dollar faces significant weakening, coinciding with rising prices of gold and silver.

Economist Peter Schiff has issued a warning about potential value losses in both the U.S. dollar and cryptocurrencies such as Bitcoin. He suggests that diversification into precious metals could be a prudent strategy.

In the meantime, global stocks, particularly in the energy sector, are expected to experience growth. This growth is anticipated to be driven by advancements in artificial intelligence and increasing demand from data centers and electric vehicles.

data.title: David Sacks: Trump wants to establish a single federal framework for AI regulation.
data.description: David Sacks, the White House's AI and crypto director, stated that one of the biggest threats to AI innovation is "outrageous" regulation. Trump's desire to establish a single federal framework for AI regulation suggests that inconsistent state-level AI regulations could lead to piecemeal policy developments. (Jinshi)

data.title: Columbia Professor Criticizes NYSE's Blockchain Tokenization Plan
data.description: A Columbia business professor has expressed skepticism about the New York Stock Exchange's blockchain tokenization initiative, describing it as vague and lacking in specific details. According to NS3.AI, the professor contends that the plan seems to aim at preserving the current financial system rather than introducing substantial innovation. This critical perspective questions the transformative potential that the NYSE's blockchain ambitions are believed to hold.

data.title: Category Labs plans to repurchase MON tokens through the open market in the first half of 2026.
data.description: Category Labs announced on the X platform that it may repurchase up to $30 million worth of MON tokens on the open market in the first half of 2026, subject to applicable regulations. The announcement stated that any repurchase will be at its own discretion and may be initiated, suspended, or terminated at any time. This announcement does not constitute a commitment to purchase a specific number of tokens, but only indicates that Category Labs will consider opportunistic repurchases based on market conditions.

data.title: A whale sold 98,328 SOL tokens, worth $12.34 million.
data.description: According to Lookonchain monitoring, the whale degen-retard.sol withdrew 99,153 SOL tokens (worth $19 million) from Binance two years ago at approximately $192 each and staked them. It has since sold off the remaining 98,328 SOL tokens ($12.34 million) using a DCA strategy, incurring a loss of over $6.6 million in SOL losses after nearly two years of staking.

data.title: Trump: I expect to sign the crypto bill soon, ensuring the U.S. becomes the global crypto capital.
data.description: In Davos, US President Trump stated that he is committed to ensuring the US remains the "global cryptocurrency capital" to unlock the innovative potential of savings and financing. Following the landmark GENIUS Act signed last year, Congress is actively developing legislation to structure the cryptocurrency market, covering various crypto assets including Bitcoin. This legislation is expected to be signed soon, opening new avenues for development for the American people.

data.title: Gold and Silver Prices Experience Short-Term Decline
data.description: Spot gold prices have seen a short-term drop of over $30, currently reported at $4,822.95 per ounce. According to Odaily, spot silver has fallen below $93 per ounce, experiencing a daily decrease of 1.65%.

data.title: Tokenization's Path to Broader Acceptance in Traditional Finance
data.description: The crypto industry frequently believes that institutional investors find products similarly to retail traders, utilizing social media and quick iterations. According to NS3.AI, Dean Khan Dhillon from RWA.xyz contends that traditional asset allocators, including pension funds and family offices, function differently. To achieve wider acceptance of tokenization in traditional finance, the crypto sector needs to create more advanced distribution strategies.
